name: Configuration Guide

tasks.title: Rewrite configuration.md
tasks.body:
Rewrite docs/humans/configuration.md as the how-it-works guide. The existing file has the right bones but needs expansion per the spec.

Read the current docs/humans/configuration.md first to preserve any content worth keeping. The rewrite should be a substantial expansion, not a light edit.

Page structure:

1. Three-Tier Directory Structure — visual tree showing system/base/, system/custom/, system/local/ with what lives in each and git status. Explain base is regenerated on init/update, custom is committed (team-shared), local is gitignored (personal). Include the tier table from the current page. Explain that prompts and rule fragments follow the same three-tier resolution: same-named files in higher tiers completely replace lower-tier versions.

2. Merge Semantics — how tiers merge (higher overrides lower). Three rules with concrete before/after examples:
   - JSON objects deep-merge recursively (override a single nested field without rewriting the whole object)
   - Arrays replace entirely (e.g., stage_order, model args)
   - Setting a field to null in a higher tier deletes it from resolved config

3. Common Customizations — practical recipes (more detail than quickstart, less than reference):
   - Model configuration: defining models as CLI commands, switching defaults, no provider SDK
   - Pipeline stages: dict format for selective override, stage_order, enabling/disabling stages
   - Class overrides: adding a custom class, overriding a built-in class prompt (link to Task Classes page)
   - Prompt overrides: placing files in custom/ or local/ to replace base prompts
   - Rule fragments: composable fragments, auto-discovery vs explicit ordering
   - Audit scopes: configuring audit behavior

4. Inspecting Config — wolfcastle config show with --section filtering, --tier for raw single-tier view, --raw to suppress defaults. Show users they can dump current config as a starting point.

5. CLI Config Commands — wolfcastle config set/unset/append/remove with examples. Dot-delimited paths. --tier flag for targeting custom vs local. Cross-reference to CLI command pages for full details.

6. Security — model-level security through CLI flags. Team enforcement via custom/config.json review. Personal loosening in gitignored local/config.json.

Cross-references: link to Config Reference (config-reference.md) for field details throughout. Link to Task Classes (task-classes.md) in the class overrides section. Link to how-it-works.md for daemon/pipeline context.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Configuration Guide is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify merge semantics examples are accurate against internal/config/merge.go
Verify CLI command syntax matches the actual commands in cmd/config/

breadcrumbs.text: Rewrote configuration.md as full how-it-works guide with six sections: three-tier structure, merge semantics with before/after examples, common customizations (models, stages, classes, prompts, fragments, audit), config inspection, CLI write commands, and security model. Cross-references config-reference.md, task-classes.md, and how-it-works.md throughout.

task-0001.objective: Rewrite configuration.md as the comprehensive how-it-works guide for Wolfcastle configuration
task-0001.what_happened: Rewrote docs/humans/configuration.md from a 123-line overview into a ~300-line guide with six sections: three-tier directory structure (visual tree, tier table, prompt/rule resolution explanation), merge semantics (three rules with concrete before/after JSON examples), common customizations (models, pipeline stages, class overrides, prompt overrides, rule fragments, audit scopes), inspecting config (config show with flags), CLI config commands (set/unset/append/remove with examples), and security (team enforcement vs personal loosening). Preserved all content from the original page while substantially expanding every section.
went_well: The merge semantics section with three concrete before/after examples should make the rules immediately obvious
Cross-references to config-reference.md, task-classes.md, how-it-works.md, and CLI pages create a connected documentation set
improvements: CLI command page links (cli/config-show.md etc) are aspirational; those pages may not exist yet
action_items: Verify CLI page links exist during the deep-linking pass task
